title: A Race Winning Strategy
text: NASCAR one top-tier racing sports North America competes F1 IndyCar top viewership spot. Approximately 3.22 million people watch race given weekend throughout season. nascaR.data package result wanting share passion sport provide option typical go-packages learning new data visualization tools. nascaR.data packed full NASCAR results dating back first Daytona Beach race 1949! Use package discover race trends across NASCAR Cup Series, Xfinity Series, Craftsman Truck Series. Answer fun questions like “driver accumulated wins overall?”, “owner best top 10 percentage Daytona?”, see manufacturer dominated series certain season. ’s , let’s strap race seats, fire engines, let’s take warm-laps.

title: A Race Winning Strategy
text: nascaR.data provides access 21 different datasets (7 per series) broken overall race results driver, owner, manufacturer season & career records. Let’s check gauges see ’s hood: Use ?nascaR.data::cup_race_data view list variable descriptions. package designed swap cup xfinity truck see data structure (variables) respective series. rather inspect driver-specific results listed season overall career? problem… easy pit stop: cup_driver_career xfinity_owner_season truck_mfg_overall. Manufacturer (mfg) results use term “overall” instead “career” used driver owner. cup_mfg_overall vs cup_driver_career

title: A Race Winning Strategy
text: drivers Top 5 wins NASCAR Cup Series? First, organize drivers descending order win. , subset keep Top 5 winningest drivers. Lastly, feed data horizontal bar chart (tweaks applied enhance visual output).  Wow! doesn’t even look like close race. Richard Petty clearly leads field 200 wins. However, let’s take drive little deeper turn account number races driver competed . compare five drivers win percentage?  Accounting Richard Petty’s 1185 races David Pearson’s 575 races, imagine many wins Pearson competed many races King.
code: cup_driver_career |>   arrange(desc(career_wins)) |>   slice_head(n = 5) |>   ggplot(aes(driver, career_wins)) +   geom_bar(stat = 'identity') +   coord_flip() cup_driver_career |>   arrange(desc(career_wins)) |>   slice_head(n = 5) |>   ggplot(aes(driver, career_win_pct)) +   geom_bar(stat = 'identity') +   coord_flip()

title: A Race Winning Strategy
text: manufacturer best win percentage season? Let’s go behind pits see manufacturers Truck Series.  clear trend emerges, though appears may 5-year clustering winning percentage. example, Dodges experienced success early 2000s, started fall exiting series. Ford seemingly gradual improvement, can clearly see success Toyota camp since joining Truck series 2004.
code: truck_mfg_season |>   ggplot(aes(season, mfg_season_win_pct, group = manufacturer, color = manufacturer)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point()

title: A Race Winning Strategy
text: Collect race winnings average money winning race changed time?  Race winnings Cup series experienced exponential growth beginning 1980s Xfinity Truck Series winnings remained relatively since 2000.
code: cup <- cup_race_data |>   mutate(series = 'Cup') |>   filter(finish == 1) |>   select(season, race, finish, money, series)  xfinity <- xfinity_race_data |>   mutate(series = 'Xfinity') |>   filter(finish == 1) |>   select(season, race, driver, money, series)  truck <- truck_race_data |>   mutate(series = 'Truck') |>   filter(finish == 1) |>   select(season, race, driver, money, series)  bind_rows(cup, xfinity, truck) |>   group_by(series, season) |>   summarize(mean_money = mean(money, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   ggplot(aes(season, mean_money, group = series, color = series)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() ## Warning: Removed 24 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range ## (`geom_point()`). ## Warning: Removed 24 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range ## (`geom_line()`).

title: A Race Winning Strategy
text: nascaR.data built R version 4.4.1 tidyverse (2.0.0), rUM (2.0.0), ggtext (0.1.2) packages used preprocess summarize data.1–5

title: Authors and Citation
text: Grealis K (2024). nascaR.data: NASCAR Race Data. R package version 1.0.0, https://kylegrealis.github.io/nascaR.data/, https://github.com/kyleGrealis/nascaR.data/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {nascaR.data: NASCAR Race Data},   author = {Kyle Grealis},   year = {2024},   note = {R package version 1.0.0, https://kylegrealis.github.io/nascaR.data/},   url = {https://github.com/kyleGrealis/nascaR.data/}, }

title: NASCAR Race Data
text: nascaR.data curated group datasets across NASCAR’s top three series: Cup, Xfinity, Trucks. 21 sets available explore & use creating tables data visualizations. Install nascaR.data : Project updates: Craftsman Truck Series data (completed: 5/23/2024) Busch/Xfinity Series data (completed: 6/3/2024) NASCAR Cup Series data (completed: 6/3/2024) data cleaning variable creation (completed: 6/16/2024) R package tidbits (completed: 6/21/2024) R package submitted CRAN (completed: 6/25/2024)

title: NASCAR Race Data
text: NASCAR one top-tier racing sports North America competes F1 IndyCar top viewership spot. Approximately 3.22 million people watch race given weekend throughout season. nascaR.data package result wanting share passion sport provide option typical go-packages learning new data visualization tools. nascaR.data packed full NASCAR results dating back first Daytona Beach race 1949! Use package discover race trends across NASCAR Cup Series, Xfinity Series, Craftsman Truck Series. Answer fun questions like “driver accumulated wins overall?”, “owner best top 10 percentage Daytona?”, see manufacturer dominated series certain season. ’s , let’s strap race seats, fire engines, let’s take warm-laps.

title: NASCAR Race Data
text: nascaR.data provides access 21 different datasets (7 per series) broken overall race results driver, owner, manufacturer season & career records. Let’s check gauges see ’s hood: {r, echo=TRUE} library(nascaR.data) Use ?nascaR.data::cup_race_data view list variable descriptions. package designed swap cup xfinity truck see data structure (variables) respective series. rather inspect driver-specific results listed season overall career? problem… easy pit stop: cup_driver_career xfinity_owner_season truck_mfg_overall.

title: NASCAR Race Data
text: drivers Top 5 wins NASCAR Cup Series? First, organize drivers descending order win. , subset keep Top 5 winningest drivers. Lastly, feed data horizontal bar chart (tweaks applied enhance visual output). {r, echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE, warning=FALSE} cup_driver_career |>   arrange(desc(career_wins)) |>   slice_head(n = 5) |>   ggplot(aes(driver, career_wins)) +   geom_bar(stat = 'identity') +   coord_flip() Wow! doesn’t even look like close race. Richard Petty clearly leads field 200 wins. However, let’s take drive little deeper turn account number races driver competed . compare five drivers win percentage? {r, echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE, warning=FALSE} cup_driver_career |>   arrange(desc(career_wins)) |>   slice_head(n = 5) |>   ggplot(aes(driver, career_win_pct)) +   geom_bar(stat = 'identity') +   coord_flip()

title: NASCAR Race Data
text: manufacturer best win percentage season? Let’s go behind pits see manufacturers Truck Series. {r, eval=FALSE, warning=FALSE} truck_mfg_season |>   ggplot(aes(season, mfg_season_win_pct, group = manufacturer, color = manufacturer)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() clear trend emerges, though appears may 5-year clustering winning percentage. example, Dodges experienced success early 2000s, started fall exiting series. Ford seemingly gradual improvement, can clearly see success Toyota camp since joining Truck series 2004.

title: NASCAR Race Data
text: Collect race winnings average money winning race changed time? ```{r, eval=FALSE,, warning=FALSE} cup <- cup_race_data |> mutate(series = ‘Cup’) |> filter(finish == 1) |> select(season, race, finish, money, series) xfinity <- xfinity_race_data |> mutate(series = ‘Xfinity’) |> filter(finish == 1) |> select(season, race, driver, money, series) truck <- truck_race_data |> mutate(series = ‘Truck’) |> filter(finish == 1) |> select(season, race, driver, money, series) bind_rows(cup, xfinity, truck) |> group_by(series, season) |> summarize(mean_money = mean(money, na.rm = TRUE)) |> ggplot(aes(season, mean_money, group = series, color = series)) + geom_point() + geom_line() ``` Race winnings Cup series experienced exponential growth beginning 1980s Xfinity Truck Series winnings remained relatively since 2000.

NASCAR Cup Series Results — cup_race_data
text: collection NASCAR's top level competitive race series results first race 1949 completion 2023 season. series undergone several name changes, including start Strictly Stock Grand National Winston Cup current NASCAR Cup Series.

title: NASCAR Cup Series Results — cup_race_data
NASCAR Cup Series Results — cup_race_data
text: data frame 97,474 rows 23 variables: season num: Racing season (1949-2023) race num: Chronological race number season (1-62) site chr: Location race (city) track chr: Race track name track_length num: Race track length, miles track_type chr: Race track surface (dirt track, paved track, road course) finish num: Finish position race start num: Start position race driver chr: Driver's name manufacturer chr: Car manufacturer (e.g., "Chevrolet", "Ford", "Toyota") car_number chr: Car number owner chr: Car owner sponsor chr: Car sponsor win num: Indicator variable win; 1 = win, 0 = win top_5 num: Indicator variable finish position 5th place better; 1 = yes, 0 = top_10 num: Indicator variable finish position 10th place better; 1 = yes, 0 = top_20 num: Indicator variable finish position 20th place better; 1 = yes, 0 = laps num: Number laps completed race laps_led num: Number laps led race status chr: Status end race (e.g., "Running", "Accident"), available money num: Total race earning (dollars) including bonus money, available. available data beginning 2016 season. pts num: Points earned race. Available beginning 1975 season. playoff_pts num: Playoff points earned race. Available beginning 2017 season.
